revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Overly detailed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Overly detailed/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Overly detailed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Overly detailed/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Overly detailed/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Overly detailed/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23524127410|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24957526818|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} {{Template shortcut|Overly|Fancruft}} {{Template redirect|Intricate detail|Excessively detailed}} {{Lua|Module:Unsubst}} This template is used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience. == Usage == ;Full page :{{Overly detailed|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Section :{{Overly detailed|section|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} ;Suppress recommendation to split :{{Overly detailed|section|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} :{{Overly detailed|nosplit=1|details=|date={{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''Date''', '''section''', '''nosplit''', and '''details''' are all optional. The "section" attribute, if included, customises the wording to apply only to a section of an article. The word "section" should be used as is, and not replaced with the name of the section. The "nosplit" parameter, if set, removes the phrase [[Wikipedia:Content forking#Article_spinoffs: .22Summary style.22 meta-articles and summary sections|spinning off]] or [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia#Recommendations for handling trivia|relocating]] any relevant information, and from the template. This is useful when details need to be removed altogether, not split off or moved elsewhere. The "details" parameter, if set, can be used to customize the message. This template should '''not''' be [[Wikipedia:Template substitution|subst'ed]]. == Notes == This template will add tagged articles to [[:Category:Wikipedia articles that are excessively detailed]], dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. == Template data == { "params": { "1": { "label": "Section", "description": "Replaces phrase \"article\" with \"section\"", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "details": { "label": "Additional details", "description": "Any additional details you would like to add to this request", "example": "an excess of advanced scientific data in the lead section", "type": "string", "suggested": true }, "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that this cleanup request was placed", "example": "March 2017", "type": "date", "autovalue": "May 2025", "required": true }, "nosplit": { "label": "nosplit", "description": "Suppresses the recommendation to split or move content", "type": "string", "suggested": true } }, "description": "Used to identify articles which, while comprehensible, contain excessive detail on about trivial subjects and need to be refocused to suit a more general audience.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "nosplit", "details", "date" ], "format": "inline" } == See also == * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] * [[Wikipedia:Fancruft]], an [[WP:ESSAY|essay]] on "fancruft". * [[Wikipedia:Handling trivia]], an essay on trivia in Wikipedia, what it is and how it should be handled. * [[Wikipedia:"In popular culture" content]], an essay on "Popular culture" articles and sections. * [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Trivia Cleanup]], a WikiProject designed to clean up long lists of trivia in articles. * [[Wikipedia:Why is BFDI not on Wikipedia?|Wikipedia:Why is ''BFDI'' not on Wikipedia?]], an essay about a highly popular web series that has no independent reliable sources for Wikipedia. * {{tl|Coatrack}}, for articles that are primarily related to totally different subjects or aspects of that subject * {{tl|Fanpov}}, for articles written from a fan's point of view. * {{tl|Long plot}}, for plot summaries which are too long or excessively detailed. * {{tl|Prose}}, a similar box that suggests converting the section into prose, rather than integrating the items into the prose of other sections. * {{tl|Trivia}}, a similar box suggesting the merging of basic trivia into prose in other sections. * {{tl|Overly detailed inline}}, for use inline for excessive or trivial detail. * {{tl|Very long}} * {{tl|Synthesis}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Cleanup templates]] [[Category:Notability and importance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
